mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, causing analysts to downgrade earnings forecasts and triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in housing starts and commercial real estate credit indicates a synchronized pullback in aggregate demand, reducing corporate revenue growth expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated industrial production reflects tight capacity utilization and strong order books, allowing firms to maintain pricing power and expand profit margins, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO exceeds the threshold, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate revenues and cash flows, providing fundamental support that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the rolling 3-year correlation between GDP growth surprises and SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing the equity risk premium and compressing valuation multiples, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite sustained inflation spikes above the threshold, or the signal's predictive accuracy does not exceed 50% in a walk-forward test spanning two decades.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms labor market resilience, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid quarterly GDP growth during early recovery phases historically precedes strong equity rallies as corporate earnings catch up to macroeconomic improvements, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during rapid growth episodes, or the signal generates false positives in more than 65% of late-cycle growth spurts.
